title: alcea gorganica
intro: Alcea gorganica, also known as the Persian hollyhock, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Iran. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Alcea gorganica include its potential to treat various conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Alcea gorganica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a topical cream or ointment to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
